nav.newCompanyConfirm: Clear the selected company in this browser and return to the goal screen? Server RAM state is not deleted — this only changes the current company in the UI.

landing.headline: One goal → a company of agents that actually runs.
landing.sub: Blueprint, roles, orchestrator ticks, approvals, and cost — wired for a clean swap to real LLMs and connectors. Sign in to spin up a demo company on your project.
landing.feat1d: Mission, KPIs, budget, and initial task graph from one prompt.
landing.feat2d: Approvals and pause/cancel before anything risky ships.
landing.feat3d: Per-agent cost and audit trail while the orchestrator ticks.

login.title: Welcome back
login.sub: Sign in to open the control plane.

settingsLlm.needSignIn: Sign in to edit LLM settings.
settingsLlm.title: LLM profile (per project)
settingsLlm.pageHint: These settings apply to the current project after you save.
settingsLlm.intro: For OpenAI use https://api.openai.com/v1. For Neurohub, if Base URL is left empty, the default production URL from the host docs is stored; override with your own host if needed.
settingsLlm.apiKey: API key (stored as plain text in MVP DB)
settingsLlm.errNoProject: No project — register and sign in with DATABASE_URL configured.
settingsLlm.msgSaved: Saved. New LLM calls use this profile (running tasks keep the old model until done).
settingsLlm.mockHint: No network calls — blueprints use the built-in deterministic generator. Best for demos and tests.
settingsLlm.hasKeyHint: A key is already stored on the server. Leave the field empty to keep it; enter a new value to replace.

factory.title: Agent Company Factory
factory.subtitle: Architectural MVP — control plane for autonomous agent companies
factory.scheduler: The MVP scheduler is tick-based: each tick processes a limited batch of tasks. “Run until idle” calls ticks in a row until the response has executed === 0 (at most {{maxTicks}} times). Company state lives in the server process RAM; after sign-in the project is picked up from factory_project_id in localStorage.
factory.howTo1: Edit the goal below (or keep the sample). At least ~10 characters so buttons stay enabled.
factory.howTo2: Review Blueprint — LLM draft of team and tasks first; you can exclude roles and rebuild.
factory.howTo3: Skip review — create the company with this prompt without the review screen.
factory.howToRam: Demo data lives in process memory: after a server restart the company disappears — expected for this MVP.
factory.blueprintOverlaySub: The LLM can take a little while — hang tight.

llmProgress.preflightStart: Checking reachability to the LLM host…
llmProgress.preflightOk: Host responded (preflight {{ms}} ms) — starting model request…
llmProgress.streamAttempt: Streaming response — you should see token counts if the gateway supports SSE.
llmProgress.streamChars: Receiving model output… {{n}} characters so far
llmProgress.jsonAttempt: Waiting for full JSON response (non-stream or fallback)…

factory.goalHint: Review Blueprint — inspect and edit the proposal before creating the company. Skip review — create the company immediately.
factory.ramExplainer: Data is not in Postgres/SQLite — only the Next.js process RAM (src/factory/store/db.ts). After npm run dev restart or HMR that resets the module, the server “forgets” companies; the browser may still hold an old id in sessionStorage — click Reset and create a company again.
factory.acceptNeedAgent: Select at least one agent before accepting.
factory.defaultMissionPrompt: Launch an autonomous B2B lead generation company for an AI-concierge service. Find target companies, enrich them, draft personalized outreach, and book qualified discovery calls. Daily budget $50. All outbound emails require human approval before being sent.

proposal.title: Blueprint proposal
proposal.teamHint: Uncheck or use ✕ — the card is marked Excluded (won’t be in Accept). Restore with the arrow. On Rebuild, the LLM prompt includes the mission, your text in the field below (if any), and excluded roles.
proposal.feedbackPh: e.g. Focus on B2B SaaS, fewer Outreach variants…
proposal.rebuildHelp1: — a new LLM call and a new proposal. The prompt always includes the mission.
proposal.rebuildWithFeedback: A Revision feedback block is added with your field text.
proposal.rebuildRolesPrefix: An instruction is added not to bring back roles
proposal.rebuildRolesNone: No excluded roles — the separate “omit roles” line is not inserted.
proposal.rebuildOverlaySub: Another LLM pass — same fun loading lines.
proposal.rebuildTitle: POST /api/proposals/:id/rebuild — new proposal; old one marked rebuilding

orchestrator.phasePausedHint: Click Resume company — otherwise the orchestrator won’t pick up tasks.
orchestrator.phaseApprovalHint: Approvals tab → Approve/Reject, then Run tick or Run until idle again.
orchestrator.phaseAwaitResumeHint: Usually right after Approve: the next tick moves them to queued — press Run tick.
orchestrator.phaseIdleHint: A tick may return executed=0 — normal when there is nothing to do.

taskHint.queuedExplain: tasks in queued don’t advance by themselves — only the orchestrator on a tick picks them up (buttons below or in the page header).
taskHint.howRunDetail: Run until idle → when approvals appear open Approvals → after Approve run a tick again. Check Audit and the Last run block above the tabs.
taskHint.companyPaused: Company is paused — ticks won’t execute tasks. Press Resume company in the header.
taskHint.agentPaused: Queued tasks belong to a paused agent: {{names}}. In Team (Overview) press Resume — otherwise the tick returns executed=0 and rows stay queued.
taskHint.sendOutreach: send_outreach_* usually moves to awaiting_approval on a tick (mock gmail.send) — then another tick after Approve.

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.afterApprove: After Approve the linked task re-enters the queue only on the next orchestrator tick. Press Run tick or Run until idle to continue execution.
